body:
Team --

We have a December 15 deadline on the FERC California market participation inquiry. Given the scope of the request and the compressed timeline, we need to establish our coordination protocol immediately.

I'd like to propose the following approach:

First, we need a clear division of labor between government affairs and regulatory compliance. Based on the sub-questions in the request, I'm thinking we split along functional lines -- government affairs handles the policy participation narrative and legislative context, while regulatory coordinates the procedural compliance documentation and factual responses.

Second, we need a document review schedule that builds in at least two rounds of revision before submission. I would suggest we target a first draft completion by December 5, internal review complete by December 10, and final submission package by December 13 to allow buffer.

Third, we need to align with legal on privilege concerns before we finalize anything.

Mary -- can you pull together a preliminary breakdown of the sub-questions and assign initial ownership? I'll coordinate on the government affairs side. Let's aim to have a joint working session set up by end of week to finalize the response structure.

Can your assistant get a call set up for Thursday or Friday this week?

Jim

Jim --

Confirmed on the division of labor. I'll take point on the procedural compliance documentation and factual response track.

I've reviewed the sub-question structure from the FERC request. Initial ownership breakdown is straightforward -- I'll assign regulatory and compliance ownership to those items falling within our functional lane. One flag: several sub-questions appear to touch both policy participation and procedural record-keeping, so we'll need to coordinate closely to avoid gaps or overlap at the boundaries. I suggest we map those jointly before we distribute.

On the review schedule, your proposed dates work provided we lock in the legal privilege review by December 6 at the latest. I'll reach out to legal directly to confirm their availability and integrate that checkpoint.

Thursday works better for me this week than Friday. Can your assistant coordinate a 90-minute block with you, me, and whoever from legal we need at the table? I'll have the preliminary sub-question ownership matrix ready for review at that session.

I'll also want to loop in the trading desk leads on any questions that touch actual market participation -- they may have supporting documentation we need to incorporate.

Mary

Mary --

Thursday works on my end. I'll have my assistant lock in the 90-minute block and get calendar invites out by EOD today. We'll target 2:00-3:30 to give everyone breathing room earlier in the day.

Your point on the boundary sub-questions is well-taken. Let's make the joint mapping of those items the first agenda item at Thursday's session so we can nail the ownership split before we distribute. I'll have the government affairs framing ready to cross-reference against your regulatory breakdown.

On the trading desk leads -- good call. I can coordinate with them on the market participation documentation once we've defined the sub-question boundaries. It may make sense to have one of their leads join the Thursday session if we think the overlap is significant enough.

One additional item: I reached out to Jeff Dasovich earlier today about the legislative context piece. He should be looped into the review cycle as well, though probably at the December 10 internal review stage rather than the working session. I'll confirm his availability and get him on the schedule.

I'll have my assistant send the invite with a proposed agenda by end of day. Please feel free to add to it.

Jim

Sounds good. I'll have the preliminary ownership matrix to you by Wednesday evening so you can review before the session.

One reminder on the boundary items -- I flagged those because a few of the sub-questions on market participant communications and internal process documentation could cut both ways depending on how we interpret the scope. We should be ready to make some call decisions at Thursday on those specifically, since the legal privilege question will turn on how we characterize them.

Looking forward to the session.

Mary

Mary --

Understood on the boundary items. I'll be prepared to make those call decisions Thursday.

One note on the legal privilege angle -- I've touched base with Paul Kaufman on the regulatory side, and he's aware we'll need a preliminary privilege assessment before we finalize anything touching communications or internal processes. He should be looped into the Thursday session as well to give us an initial read on scope.

I'll include a placeholder on the agenda for the privilege characterization decisions. That should give us enough time to work through the tricky ones before we lock in the ownership assignments.

See you Thursday.

Jim
